Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The review says GGM mutants have reduced crystalline cellulose and proposes roles in polymer spacing/cellulose crystallinity.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper explicitly states csla2/muci10 mucilage has a more compact adherent layer, reduced cellulose staining and reduced crystalline cellulose/birefringence, while rays and diffuse cellulose remain present.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the review: required for GGM production, defines a third class of phenotype, and broadly affects crystalline cellulose properties.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The review explicitly suggests the phenotype may reflect altered cellulose crystallinity rather than only polymer spacing.

corrections.reason: GDP-mannose and GDP-glucose are not stated in the paper.
